inline_comments.message: I am a bit confused about why cds-db is added in application.properties and application-dev.properties. And then any-db is added in application-test.properties?

Hi Jozsef, on application.properties and application-dev.properties I added a real life example, the configuration we wanted to use in CDS Blueprintsprocessor, but on tests I added any-db to present that it doesn't need to be any predefined value - it can be anything user configure by itself. If they wants to use cds-db, cps-db, so-db or whatever-db it's possible
Anyway, whatever we have in these files is separate from oom configuration in the helm charts. So they are only used for test or reference purpose here.

The application code should not make assumptions on external configuration overrides. Adding values to application.properties and application-dev.properties signals introduction of new behaviors/features. The purpose of this patch is just to enable users to add new databases to the configuration, rather than introducing cds-db as a default source.

Also if my memory servers me well, application.properties packaged inside the JAR is still being read, but values are overridden by the external application.properties file, which means that if you have property A in the packaged file and property B in the external, the application will load both A and B. That's why I hesitate adding new properties unless they have a purpose.

I suggest we only keep the application-test.properties in this submission.
